channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Proof of our progress, Tinubu’s aide reacts to Trump’s letter
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu’s aide reacts to Donald Trump’s commendation letter, calling it further proof of Nigeria’s progress in tackling insecurity and terrorism.
Read More: https://punchng.com/proof-of-our-progress-tinubus-aide-reacts-to-trumps-letter/

item.title: Wike’s daughter graduates with law degree from UK university
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Jazmyne Wike, daughter of FCT Minister Nyesom Wike, graduates with a law degree from the University of Southampton, UK. Read about her academic achievement
Read More: https://punchng.com/wikes-daughter-graduates-with-law-degree-from-uk-university/

item.title: Salt stars as Fire end eight-match Hundred losing streak against Brave
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Phil Salt weathered a bowling storm from England fast bowler Jofra Archer as the Welsh Fire captain led his side to a six-wicket victory over Southern Brave in The Hundred.

item.title: Saliba out for 'extended period' with back injury
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arsenal have confirmed William Saliba will be out for an extended period with a back injury.

item.title: NEMA, Kogi strengthen partnership on flood preparedness
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NEMA and Kogi State Government intensify collaboration on flood preparedness and emergency response, focusing on early warnings and community resilience.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nema-kogi-strengthen-partnership-on-flood-preparedness/

item.title: Air Peace, Etihad sign deal to connect 20 West, Central African cities
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Air Peace and Etihad Airways sign an interline agreement, expanding connectivity to 20 West and Central African cities. Book seamless travel with a single
Read More: https://punchng.com/air-peace-etihad-sign-deal-to-connect-20-west-central-african-cities/

item.title: Menzies' match is abandoned after he requires medical attention
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies has withdrawn from the World Matchplay of Darts after becoming unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith.

item.title: Minister, IGP Agree on Taskforce to Fight Power Infrastructure Vandalism
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Minister of Power, Joseph Tegbe, has asked the Nigeria Police Force to intensify protection of the country’s power infrastructure, he told the Inspector‑General of Police, Tunji Disu, during a courtesy visit to the Force Headquarters in Abuja on Tuesday. Tegbe, who was accompanied by the Ministr

item.title: FRSC arrests 121 traffic offenders in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The FRSC Anambra Sector Command arrested 121 motorists for 214 traffic offences during ‘Operation Total Compliance’ to curb violations and ensure road safe
Read More: https://punchng.com/frsc-arrests-121-traffic-offenders-in-anambra/

item.title: Yes, the AI stock selloff looks terrifying. But it might actually save the bull market.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The hottest tech trades of 2026 just got slammed — smart investors are buying anyway.

item.title: NSCDC cracks down on illegal petroleum trade, nabs two suspects in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NSCDC Anambra State Command has arrested two suspects for illegal petroleum trade and impounded 4,000 litres of suspected adulterated AGO in Onitsha.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-cracks-down-on-illegal-petroleum-trade-nabs-two-suspects-in-anambra/

item.title: Okpekpe Pays Nigerian Winners As Dunia-Backed Prize Money Reaches Athletes
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Organisers of the Okpekpe International 10km Road Race have announced the payment of prize money to Nigerian winners of the 11th edition of the race, highlighting a development they said was significant in a country where athletes have often complained of delayed payments after road races. Okpekpe P

item.title: Troops, Vigilantes Neutralise Four Bandits, Free Abducted Victims
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Security forces have eliminated four bandits and rescued two abduction victims in Aboki community of Kaiama local government, according to security reports. The post Troops, Vigilantes Neutralise Four Bandits, Free Abducted Victims appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso Submit Joint Bid To Host 2032 AFCON
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso — three countries led by military governments—are bidding to jointly host the 2032 Africa Cup of Nations. The Malian Football Federation said in a statement that officials met with Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe on Saturday in New York

item.title: NAFDAC intensifies enforcement against sachet alcohol, sub-200ml PET bottles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control, NAFDAC, has begun a nationwide enforcement operation to remove alcoholic beverages packaged in sachets and PET bottles below 200ml from circulation in line with the Federal Government’s ban on the products. The agency disclosed

item.title: 156 Rescued As NAPTIP Exposes Fake Overseas Jobs Trafficking Network
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Agency for the Prohibition of Trafficking in Persons (NAPTIP) has rescued 156 victims of human trafficking between January and July 2026, while over 300 Nigerians trafficked to Ghana and Côte d’Ivoire were repatriated in 2025, as the agency raised fresh concerns over the growing u

item.title: Dangote Resumes Sale Of Petrol In Naira, Gantry Price At N1,215 Per Litre
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The management of Dangote Petrochemical Refinery has announced that it has resumed the sale of petrol in naira. This was contained in a press statement issued by the company on Wednesday, July 22, in Abuja. The statement read: “Dangote Petroleum Refinery has resumed the sale of Premium Motor S

item.title: Chocolate City Sues Rapper Odumodublvck Over Alleged Assault of Staff Member
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Chocolate City Music has commenced legal proceedings against Nigerian rapper Odumodublvck over the alleged physical assault of one of its staff members, according to newly surfaced court documents. The alleged

item.title: Appeal Court upholds forfeiture of N293.9m, properties linked to ex-Military General Atewe
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Court of Appeal sitting in Lagos has upheld the final forfeiture of N293.97 million, investment assets and several properties linked to a retired military officer, Major General Emmanuel Jebe Atewe. In a unanimous judgment delivered by Justice Boloukuromo Moses Ugo, the appellate court dismissed

item.title: Anambra excludes murder, kidnapping, GBV suspects from plea bargain initiative
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: An official said the plea bargain arrangement would apply to all offences created under the laws of Anambra State, except murder, kidnapping, armed robbery and rape. The post Anambra excludes murder, kidnapping, GBV suspects from plea bargain initiative appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Security forces repel terrorist attack in Adamawa, recover weapon
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A combined security team comprising operatives of the Adamawa State Police Command, troops of the 144 Battalion and members of the Civilian Joint Task Force has repelled a terrorist attack on Garaha Village in Hong Local Government Area. The police command said on Wednesday night that the joint team

item.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned the U.S. government could sanction Chinese AI companies after White House officials accused Moonshot of distilling Anthropic's Fable model to develop Kimi K3.

item.title: Borno Cholera Outbreak Overwhelms Health Facilities As Cases Exceed 35,500
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), has said that healthcare facilities in Borno State are struggling to cope with a sharp rise in suspected cholera cases, as the outbreak has infected more than 35,500 people and claimed 198 lives since May 2026. MSF, in a statement on Wednesday, said the outbreak has p

item.description:

item.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Major League Soccer says it is investigating allegations of tampering by Inter Miami after the club signed Brazil midfielder Casemiro.

item.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

item.title: Guinness Declares Additional ₦7 Interim Dividend Per Share In Strong Q2 Results
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Guinness Nigeria Plc has announced its unaudited financial results for the quarter ended 30 June 2026, delivering another quarter of strong financial performance and declaring an additional interim dividend of ₦7.00 per ordinary share. Together with the ₦2.00 per share interim dividend paid earlier

item.description: The agency explained that the driver fled the scene after the crash without assisting the victim or reporting the incident to the authorities. The post LASTMA arrests tanker driver after dispatch rider died in Lagos appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria’s external reserves hits $52.73b – CBN
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN, Olayemi Cardoso has told the Senate that Nigeria's External Reserves hit $52.73billion on July 9, 2026 which was $48.88billion in January this year. The post Nigeria’s external reserves hits $52.73b – CBN appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US regulators to weigh controversial peptides backed by RFK Jr
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: FDA has questioned the evidence as demand surges for the compounds promoted for sleep, muscle growth, and anti-ageing.

item.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Arsenal confirm defender William Saliba is set to miss an "extended period" after suffering a back injury while at the World Cup with France.

item.title: Mexico City Pozole Chain Casa de Tono Explores Sale on Expansion Pitch
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Mexican family-owned restaurant chain La Casa de Toño is seeking a sale in a deal that could top $400 million and set the business up for expansion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Aston Villa have agreed a deal to sign Alejandro Garnacho from Chelsea, Sky Sports reports. The arrangement is for a loan with a conditional obligation to buy, which is easily achievable. The total package, including the loan fee, is understood to meet Chelsea’s valuation. item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has opened its 2026 recruitment portal for qualified Nigerians seeking employment in various positions across Grade Levels 07, 08, 09, 10 and 12.

item.description: Three people sustained serious injuries after a truck reportedly suffered brake failure and rammed into three vehicles along the LASU–Isheri Road at Iyana School, inward Igando, Lagos. item.title: Southwest Beats Estimates on Strong Demand, Premium Spending
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Southwest Airlines Co. provided an outlook for the full year that exceeded Wall Street’s projections, citing robust travel demand and spending on the carrier’s premium products, even as operating revenue and passenger revenue for the second quarter missed analysts’ estimates.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The naira recorded its highest appreciation against the United States dollar on Wednesday at the official foreign exchange market.Data from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) showed that the naira strengthened further on Wednesday to N1,369.64 per dollar, up from N1,375.31 per dollar recorded on Tues

The 26-year-old has penned a long-term contract with the Orange and Green, keeping him at the Stadio Pier Luigi Penzo until June 30, 2030. Adams arrives after an

item.title: Global oil prices settle at 6-week high after topping $95 a barrel, as hopes dim for de-escalation of Iran war
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices settled at six-week highs on Wednesday after the U.S. struck Iran for the 11th night in a row and President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. will bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if Tehran fires on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Pool supplier Leslie’s Inc. is considering a range of strategic options to address its debt load, including a potential Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Court Stops EFCC From Investigating Oyo Government Finances
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A Federal High Court in Ibadan has restrained the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission from proceeding with its planned investigation into the finances of the Oyo State Government, ruling that the exercise was speculative The post Court Stops EFCC From Investigating Oyo Government Finances appea

item.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

item.title: Gaza man returns home after family believed he was dead
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Hamada Al Banna reunited with his family more than a year after an explosion that left him in a coma.

item.title: Brazil Unveils New Relief for Companies Hit by US Tariffs
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brazil’s government unveiled a relief package worth 18.5 billion reais ($3.7 billion) in credit for exporters hit by new US tariffs, as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva seeks to shield key sectors from damage ahead of October elections.

item.title: 2027: Opposition Yet To Propose Any Credible Alternative Ideas – Fashola
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Former Lagos State Governor, Babatunde Raji Fashola, has said Nigeria's opposition parties are yet to present credible alternative ideas that distinguish them from the policies of the current administration ahead of the 2027 general elections. The post 2027: Opposition Yet To Propose Any Credible Al

item.title: Cholera outbreak in Borno overwhelms health facilities as suspected cases exceed 35,000
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The organisation attributed the surge to overcrowded informal settlements, limited access to safe drinking water, poor sanitation and inadequate waste management The post Cholera outbreak in Borno overwhelms health facilities as suspected cases exceed 35,000 appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: ‘Owning a pet has become a luxury’ — vets worry about higher prices and shrinking pet population, survey finds
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A Morgan Stanley survey shows that U.S. vet practices are growing sales by raising prices as they contend with fewer visits —a formula that analysts say is unsustainable.

item.title: Kano ready for state police, says governor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Kano State Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf has declared that the state is fully prepared for the establishment of state police, describing the initiative as a vital step towards strengthening internal security. The post Kano ready for state police, says governor appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Senate moves to bring foreign aid, NGO funding under legislative oversight
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday passed for second reading a bill seeking to subject foreign aid, donor-funded projects and grants received

item.title: Taraba Govt Trains 200 Firefighters To Tackle Recurring Fire Incidents
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The initiative follows repeated fire disasters across the state and the challenges associated with responding effectively to them. The post Taraba Govt Trains 200 Firefighters To Tackle Recurring Fire Incidents appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NAFDAC begins nationwide seizure of banned sachet, PET bottle alcohol
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) has commenced a nationwide enforcement operation to seize alcoholic beverages packaged in The post NAFDAC begins nationwide seizure of banned sachet, PET bottle alcohol appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Our Democracy Still Evolving, We Have A Lot To Learn – Fashola
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Former Lagos State Governor, Babatunde Raji Fashola, has described Nigeria's democratic governance as still undergoing an evolution to eventually reach maturity. The post Our Democracy Still Evolving, We Have A Lot To Learn – Fashola appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Alaafin adjusts Oro festival in Oyo over BECE exams
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Alaafin of Oyo, Oba Akeem Owoade, has approved a slight modification to this year's Oro Festival to accommodate Junior Secondary School The post Alaafin adjusts Oro festival in Oyo over BECE exams appeared first on Tribune Online.
